Speaker: Woman
Text: Mullen High School, may I help you?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Hi, do you have a... um, gym teacher?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Pardon me?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Do you have a gym teacher?

Speaker: Woman
Text: A gym teacher?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Uh-huh.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Well, yeah, we have all kinds of gym teachers.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Can I talk to him?

Speaker: Woman
Text: No, I'm sorry. They are all gone for the day.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Uh, ma'am?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Yes?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Unkind, my son there?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Well... I don't know about this year, but, uh... you can do that for next year.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Mm, I need to get him in right away.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Well, I'll tell you what.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Brother Marvin would be in charge because he's in.

Speaker: Woman
Text: in charge of admissions?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Uh, no, I don't have any brothers. Did--

Speaker: Woman
Text: Pardon me?

Speaker: LPC
Text: I don't have any brothers, neither does my son.

Speaker: LPC
Text: He's an only child.

Speaker: LPC
Text: I need to enroll in, please, if that would be feasible.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Sir, what I'm saying is Brother Marvin is in charge of admissions.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Uh-huh.

Speaker: LPC
Text: I don't have any admissions to make to you.

Speaker: LPC
Text: I admit I would like my son to go there.

Speaker: LPC
Text: What kind of lunches do you, sir, for the kids?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Well, we have cold lunches.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Mmhmm. Okay, so what time should I have him there for first-- [beep] --hour tomorrow?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Your son?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Yes.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Oh, sir, you can't bring him in.

Speaker: Woman
Text: You have to have him enrolled and registered.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Well, he is.

Speaker: LPC
Text: He can do all that tomorrow during lunch or something.

Speaker: LPC
Text: When does the class start?

Speaker: Woman
Text: I'm sorry, sir, but you need to speak with Brother Marvin first.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Um... does he give ointment to the kids?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Yes, he would, uh-huh, but you have to speak with him first.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Oh, he does deal with ointment then?

Speaker: Woman
Text: I will have to have him just.

Speaker: Woman
Text: give you a call.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Uh-huh.

Speaker: Woman
Text: Could I have your name and number, and I'll have him call you.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Okay, can I ask you one more question?

Speaker: Woman
Text: Sure.

Speaker: LPC
Text: Do you say you have cold lunches?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Do the kids get ointment sandwiches?

Speaker: Woman
Text: What kind of sandwiches?

Speaker: LPC
Text: Ointment?

Speaker: Woman
Text: No, I don't believe, so I never heard of that.
